title: HexagonDescriptor
description: Machine-readable schema for the YAML frontmatter block every repo carries at the top of its HEXAGON.md (D013). Describes stable identity/structure only — deliberately no `version` field (hand-edited versions go stale); changing state lives in `registry.entry`, sourced from git/CI. Also deliberately omits `consumedBy` (derived by control-plane by inverting `deps` across the fleet) and `ports` (deferred — `deps` + `contracts.used` are the machine projection of the port tables; prose keeps the rest).

functionalName.description: Functional name of the repo — no theme words (D002). Same shape as app.manifest's name.

kind.description: What this repo is: a runtime hexagon, a product app (D009), or a buildtime tool.

side.description: Which wall the repo sits on (D011). `ui` is the side whose repos the theme-check skips — dashboard is side: ui.

class.description: D010 risk class tier. Meaningful only when kind: app; the schema stays permissive here — policy-level enforcement (e.g. "apps must have class") belongs to the conventions validator, not this schema.

spec.description: Vault spec filename this repo implements.

decisions.description: Platform decisions (vault PLATFORM_DECISIONS.md) this repo's design follows.

deps.description: Functional names of sibling platform repos this one depends on. What dependency-direction checks (hub never imported by host) read. May be empty.

contracts.description: Omitted entirely by buildtime repos like contracts itself, which pin nothing.

pin.description: Git tag of contracts checked out.

used.description: Per-contract names consumed, not per-repo — contracts version independently and the D015 rebuild set is computed per contract from this field. Each entry is a machine-readable contract id (dot/hyphen-separated lowercase segments), not free text — this is what makes the D015 rebuild set mechanically computable.
